attributes.description: Alerts when `haproxy.requests.queued > 0` sustained. Any ongoing queue depth signals backend saturation.
attributes.name: [HAProxy OTel] Requests queued at backend

investigation_guide.blob:
## HAProxy requests queued at backend

### What fired
`haproxy.requests.queued > 0` over the window.

### Why it matters
Queued requests wait for a free backend server slot. Sustained queueing causes cascading latency and eventual timeouts.

### Triage
1. Identify which backend is queueing and why.
2. Inspect per-server max-conn saturation.
3. Look at response time for the backend.

### Remediation
- Raise per-server capacity.
- Add servers.

### Tuning
- Fires on any sustained queue.

esqlQuery.esql:
TS metrics-haproxyreceiver.otel-*
// Queued requests (waiting for server) — use LAST_OVER_TIME for current value
| WHERE haproxy.requests.queued IS NOT NULL
| STATS max_queued = MAX(LAST_OVER_TIME(haproxy.requests.queued))
  BY haproxy.proxy_name, haproxy.service_name, haproxy.addr
// Any sustained queue indicates capacity saturation
| WHERE max_queued > 0
| EVAL alert_key = CONCAT(TO_STRING(haproxy.proxy_name), "::", TO_STRING(haproxy.service_name), "::", COALESCE(TO_STRING(haproxy.addr), ""))
| SORT max_queued DESC
| LIMIT 50
